title: campomanesia repanda
intro: Campomanesia repanda, also known as the white guava, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The medicinal properties of Campomanesia repanda include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide relief from respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of Campomanesia repanda include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas, capsules, or applied topically to the skin.
